question.text: What is the maximum loan period under the Reverse Mortgage Scheme?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The maximum loan period under the Reverse Mortgage Scheme is 20 years. After this period, the borrower can continue to stay in the property but will not receive any further payments. **The Trap:** 25 years is a common home loan tenure but reverse mortgage is capped at 20 years. The borrower continues to reside in the property even after payments cease. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is specific to Series XVII. Regular home loan tenures covered in other exams have different maximums. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the client outlives the 20-year payment period, they will need alternative income sources. Plan for this contingency when recommending reverse mortgage.
